# Test-data provenance — `tests/fixtures/`

Independent reference data used to validate stem-branch against **third-party
oracles** (Evidence-Based Rigor tier 1: the answer keys are authored outside this
project). Each entry lists source, license, how it was generated, the consuming
test, and an md5.

| File | md5 |
|---|---|
| `cnlunar-lunisolar.json` | `b103a9f6653fac50c14cda0cefd55bd7` |
| `jpl-lunar-phases.json` | `4d25217bbbe130545894a9a7495f1d28` |
| `jpl-planet-positions.json` | `cb0ae861b4909b776513722599c1acf7` |
| `sweph-planet-positions.json` | `955a9dc53530289cfb58066456649f4b` |
| `sxwnl-day-pillars.json` | `107f2bc20942ed50c437d8b632677c44` |
| `sxwnl-solar-terms.json` | `ea0e0362d9a019e10727a1c84ab9cb9a` |
| `sxwnl-year-month-pillars.json` | `91f47b44babb7270b360a8052b14438e` |

## cnlunar-lunisolar.json — Chinese lunisolar calendar

- **Source:** [cnlunar](https://github.com/OPN48/cnlunar) v0.2.4, a third-party
  Python implementation of the Chinese lunisolar calendar (independent of sxwnl).
- **License:** MIT (© 2025 OPN48) — redistributable.
- **Generated by:** [`scripts/gen-cnlunar-fixture.py`](../../scripts/gen-cnlunar-fixture.py)
  (`cnlunar.Lunar(dt).lunarMonth / .lunarDay / .isLunarLeapMonth`).
- **Contents:** 2,424 entries — the 1st and 15th of every month, 1950–2050 —
  each `{g:[y,m,d], l:[lunarMonth, lunarDay], leap:bool}`.
- **Consumed by:** `tests/lunisolar-cnlunar.test.ts` — differential check of
  `gregorianToLunisolar` (month, day, leap-month flag). A second independent
  calendar oracle alongside sxwnl, so the 農曆 conversion is not validated by a
  single lineage.

## jpl-lunar-phases.json — new/full moon times

- **Source:** JPL — new- and full-moon instants. JPL ephemeris output is a U.S.
  Government work in the **public domain**.
- **Contents:** `{type:'new'|'full', dateISO, timestamp}` records.
- **Consumed by:** `tests/moon-validation.test.ts` — lunar-phase timing vs JPL.

## jpl-planet-positions.json / sweph-planet-positions.json — planet positions

- **Sources:** **JPL** (public domain) and **Swiss Ephemeris** (Astrodienst;
  the ephemeris is AGPL/commercial dual-licensed — the *positions* reproduced
  here are physical facts used as a reference oracle).
- **Contents:** `{planet, dateISO, timestamp, eclLon, eclLat}` geocentric
  ecliptic positions.
- **Consumed by:** `tests/planet-validation.test.ts`,
  `tests/de441-planet-corrections.test.ts`, `tests/vsop87d-planets.test.ts`.

## sxwnl-*.json — day/year/month pillars and solar terms

- **Source:** **sxwnl 寿星万年历** by 許劍偉 (Xu Jianwei) — an independent,
  widely-used Chinese astronomical-calendar library.
- **Generated by:** [`tests/generate-reference-data.mjs`](../generate-reference-data.mjs),
  which loads sxwnl's `eph0.js` (VSOP87 + astronomy) and `lunar.js` in a sandboxed
  VM and dumps the reference values. (The sxwnl sources are fetched to `/tmp/sxwnl`;
  they are not redistributed in this repo.)
- **Contents:** day pillars (1583–2500), year/month pillars (1900–2100), and the
  24 solar-term moments.
- **Consumed by:** `tests/cross-validation.test.ts` — stem-branch vs sxwnl for
  日柱 / 年柱 / 月柱 and 節氣 timing.

> **Note on tiers.** These are tier-1 oracles (independent third parties).
> The Rust crate's `tests/data/npm_golden.txt` is different in kind — it is
> generated from *this* project's own npm engine and is a **tier-2 port-fidelity**
> check (Rust must reproduce npm), not an independent correctness oracle.
